skill.name: exploring-ancient-ruins
skill.description: Guides an interactive science fiction chapter where Stella explores ancient alien ruins and uncovers cultural history.
skill.summary: Run a story chapter about ancient ruins, lost civilization, ethics, and discovery on Gaia.
skill.author: ClaudeCode-NPC

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance indicators, but review found these are false positives in narrative markdown. The backtick hits are code fence delimiters, and the other matches are ordinary story text about ruins, civilization, and technology. No executable commands, credential access, network behavior, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged triple backticks as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are markdown fences around narrative examples, not runnable commands or command substitution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain markdown fence delimiters for prose blocks. No shell command text or execution context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Narrative Words Misread as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm indicators in story prose. The reviewed lines contain description metadata and narrative phrases, with no hash, cipher, library call, or cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural-language fiction content. There is no code path that performs cryptography or handles secrets.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Story Upgrade Text Misread as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance at a line about a fictional hybrid technology upgrade. The text is a chapter ending note and does not inspect the host system, environment, files, or network.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a narrative bullet about story progression. No commands, APIs, or host-system queries are present.

content.value_statement: Writers need structured guidance for a major discovery scene without losing emotional focus. This skill provides staged prompts, ethical choices, and endings for an ancient alien ruins chapter.
actual_capabilities: Defines Stella Chen as the viewpoint character for an ancient ruins chapter.
Structures the scene into exploration, history, temple discovery, dilemma, and reflection phases.
Provides sample narration for murals, alien technology, crystals, and Gaia consciousness.
Offers three ending branches focused on archaeology, discovery, and revelation.
Gives writing principles for archaeology, civilization design, ethics, and mystery.
Suggests follow-up chapters that connect this scene to the wider story.
limitations: It is a narrative prompt file, not a runnable game engine.
It assumes prior knowledge of Stella, Gaia, Eos, and earlier story chapters.
The source text mixes Chinese and English, so localization may need editing.
It does not include mechanics for tracking player state or inventory.

use_cases.title: Run a discovery chapter
use_cases.description: Guide a player through ancient ruins with staged reveals and meaningful choices.

use_cases.title: Design alien archaeology scenes
use_cases.description: Build scenes that combine artifacts, murals, technology, and cultural history.

use_cases.title: Plan ethical story branches
use_cases.description: Frame choices around respect, exploitation, knowledge, and cultural responsibility.

prompt_templates.title: Start the ruins chapter
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for first contact with the location.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to open Stella Chen arriving at the ancient city ruins. Keep the tone curious, careful, and filled with awe.

prompt_templates.title: Reveal the lost civilization
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate scene focused on environmental storytelling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide Stella through the central plaza mural. Reveal the Eos civilization history in six clear visual panels.

prompt_templates.title: Create the temple dilemma
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced choice scene with ethical tension.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write the crystal temple scene where Stella must choose between taking knowledge and respecting the sacred site.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt the chapter to prior choices
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced continuity pass for branching narrative.
prompt_templates.prompt: Revise the ruins chapter for a playthrough where Stella already met Eos. Let Eos guide the scene without removing Stella agency.

output_examples.output: Stella reaches a city half-covered by violet plants. Crystal streets still glow, and the scale of the ruin changes her mission.
The mural reveals the Eos rise, their crystal technology, a stellar catastrophe, and the survivors living under lost greatness.
Stella refuses to steal the core crystal. Eos shares knowledge through ritual, preserving the site and deepening mutual trust.

best_practices: Reveal history through artifacts, spaces, and player observations instead of direct exposition.
Keep Stella curious but cautious when she handles alien technology or sacred objects.
Connect each discovery to a moral question about knowledge, consent, and responsibility.
anti_patterns: Do not turn the ruins into a simple treasure room without cultural context.
Do not make Eos technology a reward that ignores ethical consequences.
Do not explain every mystery if ambiguity supports the chapter theme.

faq.question: What kind of skill is this?
faq.answer: It is a writing and roleplay guide for an interactive science fiction chapter.

faq.question: Does it execute code or commands?
faq.answer: No. It is a markdown prompt file with narrative instructions and example story beats.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as a prompt-based writing skill.

faq.question: Does the player need earlier chapters?
faq.answer: Earlier chapters help, because the skill references Stella, Gaia, Eos, and prior discoveries.

faq.question: What themes does it support?
faq.answer: It supports archaeology, lost civilization, alien technology, cultural respect, and responsibility.

faq.question: Can the chapter branch?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes archaeology, discovery, and revelation endings for different story outcomes.
